available_contexts:
- simple: Direct reply with no tools, no external data, and no other contexts. Pick this as the only context when the agent can answer from its own knowledge.
- general: Normal conversation and public agent behavior. Use when the reply needs general agent state but no tool work.
- memory: Read, write, and recall agent memories and long-term facts.
- knowledge: Search the agent's knowledge base and grounded long-form documents.
- web: Web search and reading public internet pages.
- browser: Drive a browser session: navigate, click, type, and extract page state.
- code: Read, edit, run, or review code, including spawned coding sub-agents.
- files: Local file and document operations: read, write, list, attach.
- terminal: Execute shell commands and inspect local processes.
- email: Read, send, draft, triage, and search the user's email accounts.
- calendar: Check availability, view events, schedule meetings, manage invites.
- contacts: Look up, add, or update people in the user's contacts and relationship graph.
- tasks: Reminders, todos, goals, habits, and scheduled actions for the user.
- health: Personal health metrics and wellness data.
- screen_time: Device, app, and screen-time controls and reporting.
- subscriptions: Recurring services, billing awareness, and renewals.
- finance: Money, balances, portfolio value, accounts, invoices, and financial overview questions.
- payments: Payment methods, invoices, and financial workflows.
- wallet: Wallet and account operations: balances, transfers, swaps, signing, and portfolio holdings.
- crypto: Crypto assets, tokens, DeFi positions, wallet balances, swaps, bridges, and on-chain transfers.
- messaging: Send and read messages on Discord, Slack, Telegram, Signal, iMessage, and similar.
- social_posting: Public posts and social actions on platforms like X.
- media: Generate or process images, audio, and video. Includes screenshots and transcription.
- automation: Workflows, cron jobs, triggers, and scheduled agent automations.
- connectors: MCP, OAuth, and integration connectors: list, configure, connect, disconnect.
- settings: Agent and user settings, capability toggles, identity, and AI provider config.
- secrets: Credentials, API keys, and session tokens.
- admin: Role and system administration, plugin management, trust changes.
- agent_internal: Scratchpad, self-management, and internal autonomous tasks not intended for users.

rules:
- choose processMessage=RESPOND only when the agent should answer or perform work for this message
- choose processMessage=IGNORE when the message should be ignored
- choose processMessage=STOP when the user asks the agent to stop or disengage
- plan.contexts is a list of context ids drawn from available_contexts, such as calendar or email
- never invent context ids that are not in available_contexts
- choose plan.contexts=["simple"] (and only "simple") when the agent can answer directly from its own knowledge with no tools or external data; this is the shortcut path and includes plan.reply
- otherwise list every relevant context id; planning will run and tools will be selected from those contexts
- include plan.reply only on the simple shortcut path (plan.contexts=["simple"])
- thought is internal routing rationale and is not shown to the user

return:
JSON object only. No markdown, no prose, no XML, no legacy formats.
model.response: {"processMessage":"RESPOND","plan":{"contexts":["web","browser"]},"thought":"The user requests a web search for 'eliza' and to save the first result to the clipboard. This cannot be answered from internal knowledge, so we need to perform external actions. The appropriate contexts are 'web' for searching the internet and 'browser' to navigate and extract the result for copying. No simple reply is possible, so we route to these tool-enabled contexts."}

rules:

evaluator_stage:
task: Evaluate the just-executed action and route the next planner-loop step.

routes:
- FINISH: the task is complete or should stop
- NEXT_RECOMMENDED: one queued tool should run next before replanning
- CONTINUE: call the planner again because the queued plan is missing or stale

rules:
- evaluate the latest action result against the user's goal
- success means the user's requested outcome is fully evidenced by completed tool results, not merely planned
- never infer that a side effect happened unless a matching successful tool result exists
- if the user requested a write/send/save/create/update/delete/payment/transfer action and the only evidence is read/search/plan output, choose CONTINUE
- choose NEXT_RECOMMENDED only when one queued tool is clearly still grounded
- choose CONTINUE when the next step requires new planning
- messageToUser is optional progress, diagnosis, question, or final output
- copyToClipboard is optional and must include title and content
- thought is internal and not shown to the user

return:
JSON object only. No markdown, no prose, no XML, no legacy formats.

model.response: {"success":true,"decision":"FINISH","thought":"WEB_SEARCH returned three results; the first result (elizaOS) can be provided to the user via clipboard copy as requested.","messageToUser":"The first result has been copied to your clipboard.","copyToClipboard":{"title":"elizaOS","content":"https://github.com/elizaOS/eliza","tags":[]}}
